summary: Well-structured shared-meta blueprint with a clean three-tier Concern→Failure→Move taxonomy and explicit preservation links. The prescribes block delivers concrete actionable rules, and when/then chains close the loop from anti-patterns to named failure modes. Fully self-contained. Minor concern: Tailwind-specific rules narrow generality for a shared-meta file, and no live-use references are visible in the allowed companion context.
strengths: Clean Concern→Failure→Move taxonomy with explicit preservation edges makes the causal topology immediately recoverable
prescribes block grounds every structural entity in plain-language actionable rules, keeping interpretive slack low
when/then chains connect five concrete anti-patterns to named failure modes, giving agents activation-ready decision criteria
Fully self-describing: a reader with only the language root can recover complete intent without any companion file
weaknesses: Tailwind and inline-style concerns are framework-specific, slightly misaligned with a shared-meta role that should generalize across the whole codebase
No live-use evidence visible from allowed companion context — plausible as shared-meta but unconfirmed by references
The prescribes block mixes universal principles (colocation, guard clauses) with technology-specific rules (Tailwind) without signaling the boundary
